title: Per-model capability metadata layer (official + third-party)
prompt:
Build the foundation for task-level model selection: a granular PER-MODEL capability metadata layer, covering both official providers and third-party BYOK custom-endpoint providers.

Today capability is declared PER-AGENT only — the static hand-written table AGENT_PROVIDER_CAPABILITY in src/agent-sdk/kanban/provider-protocol.ts answers only 'generic BYOK vs vendor-native + which wire protocol'. That is too coarse to route a task to the right model. This task adds a per-MODEL layer beneath it; do NOT remove or repurpose the per-agent capability table.

Define a structured ModelCapability shape (extend the existing model metadata, don't fork it) with at least: context window, max output tokens, vision/multimodal, tool/function calling, reasoning-effort support, input/output cost, speed tier, knowledge cutoff.

Sources:
- OFFICIAL providers: derive from the SDK / bundled models.json (the omp store already exposes model metadata — see memory 'cline-to-pi-test-staleness': provider data comes from the omp store + bundled models.json). Prefer SDK-provided types/metadata over hand-redefining (per AGENTS.md TypeScript principles).
- THIRD-PARTY BYOK (custom endpoint): user-declared capability fields, persisted with the per-agent provider config (src/agent-sdk/kanban/agent-provider-config.ts). No probing in this task.

Scope: data layer + read/resolve path + wire contract additions in src/core/api-contract.ts only. NO Settings UI yet (separate task) and NO routing/selection logic yet (separate task).

Acceptance:
- A typed ModelCapability resolvable for any (agent, model) pair: official from metadata, third-party from stored declaration, sensible defaults when unknown.
- Wire contract (api-contract.ts) carries the capability so the frontend can later read it.
- Unit tests for resolution precedence (declared > metadata > default).
- web:typecheck + biome clean; respect no any types, SDK types over local redefinitions.
